seoTitle: Reprint Checks in QuickBooks Without Duplicating Entries
metaDescription: Reissue a damaged or misprinted check in QuickBooks Online by flagging it Print Later and running it through the print queue again.
intro: A paper jam or misaligned print run shouldn't mean recording a payment twice. This guide shows how to flag an existing check for reprint in QuickBooks Online and run it back through the print queue without creating a duplicate ledger entry.

steps.heading: Open the check register
steps.body: Go to Transactions, then Chart of Accounts, and click View Register on your checking account.

steps.heading: Find the check
steps.body: Scroll or use filters to locate the specific check you need to reprint.

steps.heading: Flag it for reprint
steps.body: Click the check row, click Edit, then check the Print Later box next to the check number field.

steps.heading: Save the flag
steps.body: Click Save and Close to automatically queue the check back into the print dashboard.

steps.heading: Run the print batch
steps.body: Click + New, select Print Checks under Vendors, select the re-queued check, verify the check number, and click Preview and Print.

faq.question: Will reprinting a check create a duplicate transaction?
faq.answer: No, flagging Print Later on an existing check reuses the original transaction rather than creating a new one.

faq.question: What if the check was damaged before it was cashed?
faq.answer: You may want to void the original check and issue a fresh one if it needs to be legally canceled and replaced.

faq.question: How do I check my printer alignment before printing?
faq.answer: Use the layout preview screen before printing to adjust alignment settings and avoid wasting check stock.

faq.question: Can I reprint multiple checks at once?
faq.answer: Yes, the print queue lets you select and re-queue multiple checks simultaneously for a batch print run.
